Alain-Martin Richard at Ultimatum Festival 1985-05-03

Performers And Speakers
Alain-Martin Richard

00:07

Speaks [unintelligible].

Performers And Speakers
Alain-Martin Richard

00:09

OK.

Performers And Speakers
Alain-Martin Richard

00:14

Bon, il s’agit d’un texte qui est un texte charnel, traite par ordinateur. A la partition, vois imprimante toast melba et popsicle.

Performers And Speakers
Alain-Martin Richard

01:39

Performs piece, speaks, drones, and pants [underscored by vocalizations, overlapping speech, and electronic mix].

Performers And Speakers
Alain-Martin Richard

03:37

Continues piece.

Performers And Speakers
Alain-Martin Richard

04:57

Continues piece, speaks, drones, and groans [underscored by vocalizations, overlapping speech, and electronic mix].

Performers And Speakers
Alain-Martin Richard

05:59

Continues piece, speaks, drones, grunts, and pants [underscored by vocalizations, scratching sound, screams, and other electronic sounds].

Performers And Speakers
Alain-Martin Richard

07:27

Continues piece, growls, speaks, and vocalizes [underscored by vocalizations, distorted speech, and electronic mix].

Performers And Speakers
Alain-Martin Richard

08:04

Continues piece, grunts, drones, vocalizes, pants, and giggles [underscored by vocalizations, distorted speech, slurping sounds, lip smacking sounds, and electronic mix].

Alan Lord

Alan Lord

03:45

Alan Lord

04:53

Merci pour la meilleure sonneau qu’avant [speech overlaps with applause].

Alan Lord

Alan Lord

07:28

Alan Lord

08:47

Alan Lord

09:20

Alan Lord

11:43

Merci, merci [speech overlaps with end of music].

Performers And Speakers
Alan Lord

12:05

Musique du Boys du Severe qui vont jouer Vendredi soir… et a la voie Josee Thibeault vendredi soir ici meme au… au Foufounes Electriques [over top

Performers And Speakers
Alan Lord

12:30

Performs "Ignoring the Future Today", speaks rhythmically [English] [underscored by electric drums, synth, and electric keyboard].

Performers And Speakers
Alan Lord

16:20

Continues "Ignoring the Future Today", underscore drops out.

Performers And Speakers
Alan Lord

16:24

Thank you.

Performers And Speakers
Alan Lord

16:35

Merci, merci, merci, merci. Il faut comprendre que, il faut comprendre que j’aimerais ca pouvoir ecrire en francais, mais j’ecris en anglais, bon,

15:20

Boys Du Severe

Boys Du Severe

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe

17:49

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

19:05

Hey, Alan Lord. Hey, come on. [Echoing].

Unknown Speaker

19:08

Let’s improvise something.

Boys Du Severe Member

19:10

You have to play the guitar now.

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

19:12

Clapping.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

19:15

His name is Alan Lord. He’s a friend.

Performers And Speakers
Unknown Speaker

19:18

This is Alan Lord.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

19:20

Speaks [Spanish extra-poetic speech].

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

19:29

Shouts [Spanish].

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

19:44

Bon ben.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

19:44

Continues speaking [unintelligible].

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

19:51

Du drame s’il vous plait.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

19:58

Alright.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

20:05

This is all brand new.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

20:08

Do you wanna do it?

Performers And Speakers
Unknown Speaker

20:09

And that’s great.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

20:15

Setting and Tech: Technologie retrogade [cowbell], mais euh [voice effect], on voit que encore malgre tout, avec tout le signal analogue comme ca que [echo effect] l’on possede et que l’on manipule, il y a encore des gens mal pris que nous [echo effect]. Ah ouais. Jouer pas avec mes ners la parce que je’n peux plus la, je’n suis plus capable la, je vais craquer la. Okay. [Distorted echo effect].

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

20:40

Euh, tu veux te rire de moi, toi ?

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

20:46

Mais c’est bien cette machine la, j’en veux une.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

20:55

El maestro.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe Member

21:04

Echo, echo.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe

21:25

Performs piece, vocalist sings atop electronic beat [English].

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe

21:45

Continues piece.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe

21:59

Continues piece, second vocalist joins.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe

22:56

Continues piece, vocalists resume singing.

Performers And Speakers
Boys Du Severe

24:30

Continues piece, vocalists resume singing.

20:11

Denis Vanier at Ultimatum Festival 1985-05-04

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

00:01

[Unintelligible] dans la salle, hein ?

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

00:06

Ca n'est pas un gros probleme.

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

00:12

Speaks [unintelligible].

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

00:17

Okay. Une chanson pour Pagliaro. Que j'ai fait pour Paglio que j'ai jamais chante evidemment. C'est trop epe, qui dit…

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

00:24

Speaks [unintelligible].

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

00:26

Eugh. Et j'ai commence par [maybe:Rafenbag], je fini par Pagliaro, bon.

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

00:32

Il y a une personne qui veulent chante.

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

00:33

Il y a une personne qui veulent chante.

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

00:35

Mais [maybe:Janice] a envi la.

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

00:36

Bon b'en elle restera la. Bon il s'appelle "Les baisers". Chanson pour Jarry et le group [maybe:Offenbach].

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

00:45

Reads "Les baisers" [French].

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

02:23

Speaks [unintelligable].

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

02:30

Viens tu ? Viens tu, ou t’a peur ?

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

02:34

Speaks [unintelligable].

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

02:35

Bon ben viens pas. Vient en.

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

02:35

Oui je le veux. Je [maybe:film] le reste. Non, mais, esti… Si t'es pas capable, dis les pas.

Performers And Speakers
"Marie"

02:37

Speaks [unintelligable].

Performers And Speakers
"Marie"

02:48

J'ai pas besoin du livre.

Denis Vanier

"Marie"

Denis Vanier

Apres Marie, c'est l'electrolyse masculine. Ca c'est pas loin de ou ce qu'on est la. C'est a [unintelligable].

Denis Vanier

04:13

Reads "L'électrolyse masculine" [French].

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

06:17

Denis Vanier

06:24

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

06:53

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

07:15

Ca c'etait pour [unintelligible], c'est cent pourcent.

Denis Vanier

07:24

Sharp breath.

Performers And Speakers
Denis Vanier

07:28

Bon. Le dernier. Blackstrap, Vous conaissez-vous ca? Blackstrap–

Denis Vanier

07:36

Par a…

07:38

Performers And Speakers
Genevieve Letarte

01:36

Genevieve Letarte

03:12

Continues "Petite Nuage et Betelgeueuse", speaks rhythmically and sings [underscored by electronic percussion, synth, electric guitar, and keyboard].

Performers And Speakers
Genevieve Letarte

08:12

Continues "Petite Nuage et Betelgeueuse", speaks [underscored by static, high-pitched ringing, and low frequencies].

Performers And Speakers
Genevieve Letarte

09:17

Continues "Petite Nuage et Betelgeueuse" [underscored by high-pitched ringing and synth melody] .

Performers And Speakers
Genevieve Letarte

11:02

Continues "Petite Nuage et Betelgeueuse", underscore shifts [electric guitar, electronic ringing sounds, percussion, synth, bass, trombone].

Performers And Speakers
Genevieve Letarte

14:47

Continues "Petite Nuage et Betelgeueuse", speaks and sings, underscore shifts [guitar, bass, trombone, keyboard, synth].

Performers And Speakers
Genevieve Letarte

18:04

Continues "Petite Nuage et Betelgeueuse", speaks and sings [underscored by synth, bass, trombone, and cow bell].

Unknown Speaker

21:49

Merci.

Herbert Huncke at Ultimatum Festival 1985-05-05

Performers And Speakers
Alan Lord

00:06

J’espere, j’espere un jour que on peut dire 'Herbert Huncke' puis on l’appreciera pleinement son avoir a faire des deferences a la Beat Generation, Kerouac, Ginsberg, Burroughs, tous ca. Maintenant je la fait une derniere fois, la prochaine fois c’est Herbert Huncke, tout seul.

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

00:25

Alright!

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

00:29

Okay, well let me just say first before we start, all, what has happened this evening [unintelligible] a hard act to follow [unintelligible] oh well, alright, in the mic he says. Okay, can you hear now? Yes. See I have a problem with this lighting situation here, regardless of all that. I lost my place. How do you like that?

Performers And Speakers
Unknown Speaker

00:59

You have to talk into the mic.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

01:01

I will in just a minute. Some people can hear me though.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

01:07

Are you satisfied now? Okay. I really wanna start off with one particular story here because I feel that it will fit into the general theme of the so-called gathering or festival whatever, group of creative people doing things, trying to do things, young people. It’s very very encouraging for an old man like me, you know I want to think that things have progressed [laughs] a little. Boy.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

01:57

Well, here, I wasn’t gonna read this one, but I will. I’ll start anyway so everything is under control. It’s called "Cuba". It’s a rather strange story in that the individual involved was someone I met and felt somewhat sorry for if I may be permitted to feel sorry about anybody.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

02:19

Reads "Cuba" from The Evening Sun Turned Crimson [brief moments of laughter throughout].

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

09:39

Sorry, it’s not the one I want, oh I can’t do it now [unintelligible].

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

09:47

Thank you [off mic], let me see if I can find the one I intended to start out with. "Alvarez". One, let’s see, one fifty one, okay, we’ll get there, hell or high water [laughs], ah, there [laughs]. This is a, I feel it’s a very, very sad story mysel - woops. "Alvarez".

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

10:14

Reads "Alvarez" from The Evening Sun Turned Crimson.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

10:52

Continues reading.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

24:55

Okay.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

25:01

Kay.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

25:06

[unintelligible].

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

25:10

Well wait a minute. Okay [speaks over top of audience applause and cheers].

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

25:16

You want a rest period, no?

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

25:18

No.

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

25:19

Yeah.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

25:20

Not really [laughs]. I’m a ham.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

25:26

I’d like to find something just for [unintelligible] [off mic].

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

25:32

[unintelligible].

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

25:33

Anyway.

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

25:35

Muffled speech.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

25:36

Reads "Russian Blackie" from The Evening Sun Turned Crimson [muffled audience chatter throughout].

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

28:14

No, it’s alright, if they wanna talk, let ‘em.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

28:16

Continues reading [muffled audience chatter throughout].

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

34:36

Boy, I can’t see worth a damn anymore.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

34:38

Continues reading [muffled audience chatter throughout].

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

37:05

Somebody bored?

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

37:06

No.

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

37:08

Some people can’t understand it.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

37:10

It’s alright, if they’re bored they’re bored.

Performers And Speakers
Herbert Huncke

37:13

28:19

Bon, Bon soooooooooooir [modulation], bon soir! En attendan the John Giorno baaaaaaaand [echo: baaaand].

Performers And Speakers
Alan Lord

28:32

Pendent que les techniciens vont defiler le renfilage et vice versa [echo], on va visioner le film de Napoleon Moffat qui s’appelle Framed-d. Qui est originalement un super huite qui a ete transfer a trois–three quarter inch, trois quarts video. C’est un film excellent pis un des meilleures… Pour cinq minutes vous avez le poesie visuel plein de geule, okay?

Josee Yvon

Josee Yvon

Karl Jirgens at Ultimatum Festival 1985-05-05

Performers And Speakers
Alan Lord

00:02

Maintenant, uh, Karl Jirgens de la revue Rampike, Toronto.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

00:42

Le langage de silence et la violence.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

01:01

Guttural shout.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

01:18

Guttural shout.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

01:23

The language of silence is violence.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

01:32

Clapping sounds.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

01:40

Hi folks.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

01:46

Can't hardly see it.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

01:52

[Off mic]. Thanks for everybody to come out. I've really enjoyed this show so far. Can we get a little bit lower lights here on my face. [Unintelligible]. Thanks, yeah, that's good. Uh. Yeah. Okay.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

02:10

[On mic]. He said talk in the mic. [Laughs]. Um. Yeah, I'd like to thank Alan Lord and especially Joe. Everybody. Um. I'd like to begin tonight by talking about the pieces that I won't be doing. Uh. That's because there were a lot of well-intentioned people who wanted to make it out here tonight but they couldn't make it so I'm dedicating this to them. Can we have the lights here in the centre, is that possible? Okay. Don't worry about it. Actually, uh, I was gonna do a poem called "Le Moncole de Mon Oncle", but uh, I think that's been done by somebody. Uh, I was gonna do a zen piece called "My Karma Ran Over Your Dogma".

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

02:58

And maybe a, maybe a rap piece 'cause I hear rap is popular though I haven't heard any in Montreal yet. It's called uh, it's got a long title: "He Was a Winnebago Dego Living Down in Tobego Eatin' a Tomato, Big Fat Tomato, it Was a Deglo Winnebego in the Bushes of Tobego". Like, the title's almost as long as the piece but [coughs]. Then I was gonna do a, I heard Sylvere's here so I thought maybe what would be appropriate would be a social realist piece, you know so I have one called "He Got a Temporary Permanent Job at Permanent Trust But He Really Wanted a Permanent Temporary Job at the Dairy Queen".

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

03:40

And uh, I was gonna do a piece called "Address to America". It's actually a triple pun, it's 'address' as in US mail or 'a dress' as in US female or 'address' as in I'm talking to you.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

03:57

Starts reciting "Address to America" [English].

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

04:32

But I won't do that either because -

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

04:35

you've prob'ly heard that.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

04:39

So instead, I'm gonna tell you about an insane friend of mine. He's, how you say, cuckoo?

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

04:52

Recites poem [English].

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

06:09

Resumes recitation.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

07:05

Recites poem [French] [unclear if it's the same piece from before recording cuts out].

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

07:14

This one is called "The Achilles Truth". It's a little bit long, but I think you might like it.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

07:22

Recites "The Achilles Truth" [English].

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

09:16

Coughs.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

09:17

Resumes recitation.

Performers And Speakers
Audience Member

20:20

Laughs.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

20:34

Recites poem [French].

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

20:48

Speaks [unintelligible].

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

20:49

Okay. Just to wrap it up here, I'd like to do first off a very short poem.

Performers And Speakers
Unknown Performer

21:07

Po! [probably Karl Jirgens].

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

21:09

Thank you.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

21:12

I'd like to do, uh, a longer poem now.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

21:18

This one. I'll just wrap up the show with this. This one is, uh, dedicated to a South Seas mammal called the dugong. It's uh, a creature that's facing extinction now because uh, there's a lot of people down south that eat this animal and uh, it has no natural self defense mechanisms, no teeth or claws, so its very easy prey and uh, I was watching a program, sort of like Jacques Cousteau on tv one day and thinking, you know, about these poor dugongs and how they're gonna go extinct.

Performers And Speakers
Karl Jirgens

21:49

Recites poem [English].

Richard Martel at Ultimatum Festival 1985-05-05

Performers And Speakers
Richard Martel

00:04

Un, un.

Performers And Speakers
Richard Martel

00:17

Euh je vais faire trois pieces. Le premiere s’appelle le "Subordination." Le deuxieme va s’appeler "Insubordination." Et la troisieme, euh, s’appelle "L'expérience de l'art comme disponibilité du temps." Et il y aura peut-etre des petits trucs entre les trois.

Performers And Speakers
Richard Martel

00:51

Performs "Subordination" [French].

Richard Martel
